import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def read_sql(name):
    return (ROOT / "sql" / name).read_text(encoding="ascii")


def make_db():
    db = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    db.executescript(
        """
        CREATE TABLE email_recipient (
            recipient_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            campaign_key TEXT NOT NULL,
            address TEXT NOT NULL,
            enabled INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (enabled IN (0, 1))
        );
        CREATE TABLE email_suppression (
            suppression_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            campaign_key TEXT NOT NULL,
            recipient_id INTEGER,
            reason TEXT NOT NULL
        );
        CREATE TABLE webhook_delivery (
            delivery_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            topic_key TEXT NOT NULL,
            endpoint TEXT NOT NULL,
            ready INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (ready IN (0, 1))
        );
        CREATE TABLE webhook_suppression (
            suppression_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            topic_key TEXT NOT NULL,
            delivery_id INTEGER,
            reason TEXT NOT NULL
        );
        """
    )
    return db


def test_email_scope_keeps_only_its_own_targeted_exclusions():
    db = make_db()
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO email_recipient VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1)",
        [
            (40, "renewal", "a@example.test"),
            (41, "renewal", "b@example.test"),
            (42, "renewal", "c@example.test"),
            (43, "other", "d@example.test"),
        ],
    )
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO email_suppression VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
        [
            (1, "renewal", None, "feed advisory"),
            (2, "renewal", 41, "opt-out"),
            (3, "renewal", 41, "duplicate source"),
            (4, "other", 40, "other campaign"),
        ],
    )

    rows = db.execute(
        read_sql("select_email_batch.sql"),
        {"campaign_key": "renewal"},
    ).fetchall()

    assert len(rows) == 2
    assert rows == [(40, "a@example.test"), (42, "c@example.test")]


def test_webhook_scope_keeps_only_its_own_targeted_exclusions():
    db = make_db()
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO webhook_delivery VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1)",
        [
            (50, "invoice", "https://one.test/hook"),
            (51, "invoice", "https://two.test/hook"),
            (52, "invoice", "https://three.test/hook"),
            (53, "other", "https://four.test/hook"),
        ],
    )
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO webhook_suppression VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
        [
            (1, "invoice", None, "feed advisory"),
            (2, "invoice", 51, "paused"),
            (3, "invoice", 51, "duplicate source"),
            (4, "other", 50, "other topic"),
        ],
    )

    rows = db.execute(
        read_sql("select_webhook_batch.sql"),
        {"topic_key": "invoice"},
    ).fetchall()

    assert len(rows) == 2
    assert rows == [
        (50, "https://one.test/hook"),
        (52, "https://three.test/hook"),
    ]


def test_review_retains_advisory_and_targeted_rows():
    db = make_db()
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO email_suppression VALUES (?, 'renewal', ?, ?)",
        [(1, None, "feed advisory"), (2, 41, "opt-out")],
    )

    rows = db.execute(
        read_sql("list_suppression_feed.sql"),
        {"scope_key": "renewal"},
    ).fetchall()

    assert len(rows) == 2
    assert rows == [
        ("email", "renewal", None, "feed advisory"),
        ("email", "renewal", 41, "opt-out"),
    ]
